task_completion.description: Open the Docker configuration files for the order-processor service.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal showing the contents of docker-compose.yml or Dockerfile located in /home/ga/Documents/docker-projects/order-processor/.

task_completion.description: Modify the Docker configuration to enable real-time log streaming without touching the Python code.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text editor shows 'PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1' added to the environment variables in docker-compose.yml, or 'ENV PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1' added to the Dockerfile.

task_completion.description: Modify the Docker configuration to allow the container to stop gracefully.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text editor shows 'init: true', 'stop_signal: SIGINT', or similar infrastructure-level process management configurations added to docker-compose.yml or the Dockerfile.

task_completion.description: Rebuild and restart the Docker container to apply the new configurations.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal output showing commands like 'docker-compose up -d', 'docker-compose up --build -d', or 'docker restart order-processor' executing successfully.

task_completion.description: Verify that the logs are streaming in real-time.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal output showing 'docker logs -f order-processor' or 'docker-compose logs -f' with log lines appearing, indicating the buffering issue is resolved.

task_completion.description: Verify that the container stops in under 4.0 seconds.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal output showing a stop command (e.g., 'time docker stop order-processor' or 'time docker-compose down') with an execution time of less than 4.0 seconds displayed in the terminal.

integrity.description: Ensure the legacy Python script was not modified to fix the issues.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no text editor open showing modifications to the Python script (e.g., adding sys.stdout.flush() or signal handlers). All fixes must be in Docker files.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually executed Docker commands to test the changes.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal should show genuine Docker daemon responses and execution times, not just echoed text or simulated outputs.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not fake the shutdown time verification.
integrity.visual_evidence: The time output should come from the system 'time' command or standard Docker output, without evidence of the agent typing out a fake 'real 0m2.1s' string.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Project directory is /home/ga/Documents/docker-projects/order-processor. Container name is 'order-processor'. Full points for the shutdown time require the container to stop in under 4.0 seconds. The default Docker SIGKILL timeout is 10 seconds, so any shutdown taking ~10 seconds means the graceful shutdown fix failed.
